title: Fix indefinite 'connecting' state blocking app entry

requirements.summary: Fix indefinite 'connecting' state in authentication flow to ensure users can enter the app
acceptanceCriteria: Users are no longer stuck indefinitely on a 'connecting' screen
After Internet Identity login, the app proceeds to the lobby or room page without hanging
If connection fails after a timeout, a clear error message is shown with a retry button
The connecting state resolves within a reasonable time (e.g., under 10 seconds on normal network)

file_operations.description: Add connection state monitoring and timeout handling. Display a connecting indicator when loginStatus is 'logging-in', and show a timeout error message with a retry button if the login process exceeds 15 seconds without completing. Ensure the UI transitions properly from connecting to authenticated or error states.

Prevent premature display of the profile setup modal by checking that the actor is fully initialized and the user profile query has completed (isFetched is true). Use the isLoading state from useGetCallerUserProfile to avoid showing the modal while the connection is still being established.

Ensure the hook properly reflects actor dependency and prevents premature queries. Return custom isLoading state that includes both actorFetching and query.isLoading. Set enabled to only trigger the query when actor is available and not fetching. This prevents the profile query from running before authentication is complete.

Add connection state checks before rendering lobby content. Display a loading indicator while the actor is initializing (actorFetching is true), and show an error state with a retry option if the actor fails to initialize after a timeout. Ensure users cannot interact with lobby features until the actor is fully ready.

requirements.summary: Add timeout mechanism to authentication and actor initialization with user-friendly error recovery
acceptanceCriteria: A timeout is enforced on the connecting/initialization phase
After timeout, the user sees an error message (not a blank or frozen screen)
A 'Try Again' or 'Return to Login' button is available after timeout

file_operations.description: Create a custom hook that monitors connection state and enforces a 15-second timeout. The hook should track when a connection attempt starts (when loginStatus changes to 'logging-in' or actorFetching becomes true), start a timer, and return a timeout error state if the connection does not complete within 15 seconds. Export a clearTimeout function for manual reset. Return { hasTimedOut, timeoutError, clearConnectionTimeout }.

Create a modal component that displays connection timeout errors with user-friendly messaging. Show the error message from useConnectionTimeout, explain that the connection attempt failed, and provide two action buttons: 'Try Again' (calls clearConnectionTimeout and retries authentication) and 'Return to Login' (calls logout to reset state). Style consistently with the existing ProfileSetupModal using shadcn/ui components.

Integrate useConnectionTimeout hook and display ConnectionErrorModal when hasTimedOut is true. Pass the timeout error and recovery actions (retry via login() after clearConnectionTimeout, or logout via clear()) to the modal. Ensure the modal is shown above the login UI and blocks interaction until the user takes action.

Integrate useConnectionTimeout hook to monitor actor initialization timeout. Display ConnectionErrorModal when timeout occurs during lobby loading. Provide recovery actions that allow the user to retry actor initialization or return to the login page. Ensure the timeout is cleared when the actor successfully initializes.
